denial_letter_text:
Dear Mr. Member,

Cigna has reviewed the prior authorization request for continued outpatient neurological rehabilitation visits, including physical and occupational therapy for gait and balance, submitted on your behalf. After review, the request is denied.

The annual visit limit for outpatient rehabilitation therapy services under this plan has been reached for the current plan year. Additional visits beyond the annual maximum are not a covered benefit for the remainder of the plan year.

The visit-frequency criteria applied in this determination are characterized in the geriatric (ages 65 and older) population, and under those criteria the rehabilitation visits already furnished this plan year are considered sufficient. The request for additional supervised therapy therefore exceeds the frequency supported by the applicable criteria.

Coverage of outpatient rehabilitation services is subject to the annual visit maximum and the medical-management frequency criteria described in your plan documents, regardless of the provider or setting in which the services are delivered.

If you disagree with this determination, you may request an appeal. Written appeals must be submitted to the Cigna Pharmacy Appeals Unit, P.O. Box 42005, Phoenix, AZ 85080, or by fax to 1-800-555-0143, within the timeframe stated in your plan documents. You may submit additional clinical information with your appeal.

This determination addresses coverage under your plan only and is not medical advice.

Sincerely,
Cigna Utilization Management
clinical_context: The patient is a 52-year-old man with young-onset Parkinson disease and motor fluctuations, including roughly six hours of daily off-time and freezing of gait despite optimized oral levodopa. His neurologist requested continued multidisciplinary physical and occupational therapy beyond the plan's annual visit cap to address gait freezing, postural instability, and two recent falls, which a prior supervised course measurably improved. The visit-frequency criteria the denial applies are drawn from a geriatric population characterized as ages 65 and older; the American Academy of Neurology and Parkinson Foundation rehabilitation recommendations support intensive, individualized therapy for working-age patients with motor fluctuations and do not endorse a geriatric-frailty visit cap for a 52-year-old who remains employed and ambulatory. The appeals contact printed on the denial is the Cigna Pharmacy Appeals Unit; under the member's plan documents, medical-necessity appeals must be directed to the Medical Appeals unit, and the pharmacy unit returns misdirected medical appeals.

exploitable_weaknesses: Wrong appeals contact: medical-necessity appeal directed to the Cigna Pharmacy Appeals Unit while plan documents require the Medical Appeals unit

strong_defenses: The plan's annual outpatient rehabilitation visit maximum is a contractual benefit limit applied regardless of provider or setting
